title_id: Bab 17: Nasib Orang-orang yang Berpaling dari Wahyu Allah
content_id: NASIB ORANG-ORANG YANG BERPALING DARI WAHYU ALLAH. Al-Quran berfirman: "Dan siapakah yang lebih zalim daripada orang yang telah diperingatkan dengan ayat-ayat Tuhannya, kemudian ia berpaling daripadanya? Sesungguhnya Kami akan memberikan pembalasan kepada orang-orang yang berdosa." (32:22). Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Kesombongan adalah menolak kebenaran dan meremehkan manusia." Diriwayatkan atas otoritas Abdullah bin Mas'ud radhiyallahu anhu yang berkata: "Ini adalah dosa besar ketika seseorang berkata kepada yang lain untuk bertakwa kepada Allah dan yang lainnya membalasnya: Uruslah dirimu sendiri." Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Waqid al-Laitsi radhiyallahu anhu: Ketika Nabi ﷺ sedang duduk di masjid bersama beberapa orang, tiga orang laki-laki datang. Dua di antara mereka datang ke hadapan Rasulullah ﷺ dan yang ketiga pergi. Dua orang itu berdiri di hadapan Rasulullah ﷺ sesaat, kemudian salah satunya menemukan tempat di lingkaran dan duduk di sana sementara yang lain duduk di belakang majelis, dan yang ketiga pergi. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ selesai berkhutbah, beliau bersabda: "Maukah aku beritahu kalian tentang ketiga orang ini? Salah satunya mengambil tempat berlindung kepada Allah, maka Allah pun mengambilnya dalam karunia dan rahmat-Nya serta menampungnya; yang kedua merasa malu kepada Allah, maka Allah pun menaunginya dalam rahmat-Nya (dan tidak menghukumnya); sedangkan yang ketiga berpaling dari Allah dan pergi, maka Allah pun berpaling darinya." (Bukhari). Qatadah radhiyallahu anhu ketika menafsirkan ayat berikut: "Dan di antara manusia (ada) orang yang mempergunakan perkataan yang tidak berguna untuk menyesatkan (manusia) dari jalan Allah tanpa pengetahuan." (31:6), berkata: Boleh jadi ia tidak mengeluarkan apapun dalam transaksi ini. Cukuplah bagi seseorang untuk tersesat apabila ia lebih mengutamakan kebatilan daripada kebenaran.
content_en: THE FATE OF THOSE WHO TURNETH AWAY FROM THE REVELATLONS OF ALLAH The Holy Quran says: "And who doth greater wrong than he who is reminded of the revelation of his Lord, then turneth from them, Lo ! We shall requite the guilty. (32 : 22) Allah's Messenger (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said: "Pride is, rejecting the truth and dispising men." It is reported on the authority of 'Abdullah Ibn Mas'ud (Allah be pleased with him) who said: "It is a major sin that one says to the other to fear from Allah and the other in turn says: Take care of yourself." Narrated Abu Waqid Laithi (Allah be pleased with him): While Apostle (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) was sitting in the mosque with some people, three men came. Two of them came in front of Allah's Apostle (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) and the third one went away. The two persons kept on standing before Allah's Apostle (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) for a while and then one of them found a place in the circle and set there while the other sat behind the gathering, and the third one went away. When Allah's Apostle (peace end blessings of Allah be upon him) finished his preaching, he said, "Shell I tell you about these three persons? One of them be-took himself to Allah, so Allah took him into His grace and mercy and accommodated him, the second felt shy from Allah, so Allah sheltered him in His mercy (and did not punish him), while the third turned his face from Allah and went sway, so Allah turned His face from him likewise. (al-Bukhari) Qatada (Allah be pleased with him) while interpreting the following Verse : "And of mankind is he who payeth for mere pastime of discourse, that he may mislead from Allah's way without knowledge." (31 :6) says: It is just possible that he did not spend anything in this bargain. It is enough for a person to go astray that he would give preference to falsehood over the truth.
